返回本批次

高性能 Git

High Performance Git

gitperf.com 作者 gnabgib 分数 138 评论 28 2026/04/28 08:32
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Git 看起来像一个版本控制工具。它也是一个内容寻址数据库、文件系统缓存、图形遍历器和传输协议。本书介绍的是这些层以及每一层的性能成本。它从对象、引用、索引和历史遍历开始，然后向外移动到包文件、维护、稀疏工作树、部分克隆、传输、存储库规模、诊断、配置和恢复。它是为需要 Git 在存储库、历史记录和团队规模不断扩大时保持快速运行的工程师编写的：构建和 CI 工程师、monorepo 所有者、开发人员体验团队，以及当简单的解释不起作用时最终调试奇怪的 Git 行为的人。第 0 节·简介 简介 第 I 节·基础 为什么 Git 变慢，Git 存储什么，以及引用和索引如何引导它。为什么 Git 性能很重要 Git 的核心数据模型参考、HEAD、参考日志、索引第 II 节 · 历史和重写 Git 如何遍历历史以及重写命令如何在不改变提交的情况下重塑历史。修订和历史遍历合并、变基、择优挑选、重写第三部分·存储和本地规模对象存储、索引成本、维护以及缩小本地状态的技术。松散对象、打包文件、增量压缩 作为性能结构的索引 提交图、布隆过滤器、MIDX、位图 Git GC 和维护 稀疏检出和稀疏索引 第 IV 节 · 大型存储库操作、传输和规模 克隆形状、传输策略、工作树并行工作、存储库大小和引用规模。部分克隆和 Promisor 远程标量、预取、大型存储库工作树克隆、获取、推送、协议 v2 捆绑包和捆绑 URI 减少存储库大小大型引用集：文件、打包引用、Reftable 和 git 引用第五节 · 诊断和恢复如何检测 Git、找到慢速层、应用高杠杆设置以及在存储库实际错误时进行恢复。检测 Git 查找和修复缓慢的 Git 配置手册 恢复和修复 回溯事项 尾声：代理循环中的 Git 附录：兼容性指南 附录：虚拟化工作树的方法 Git 术语表 下载 PDF 格式的整本书

Original Summary

  1. Git looks like a version-control tool. It is also a content-addressed database, a filesystem cache, a graph walker, and a transfer protocol. This book is about those layers and the performance costs of each one. It starts with objects, refs, the index, and history traversal, then moves outward into packfiles, maintenance, sparse working trees, partial clone, transport, repository scale, diagnosis, configuration, and recovery. It is written for engineers who need Git to stay fast as repositories, histories, and teams get larger: build and CI engineers, monorepo owners, developer-experience teams, and the people who wind up debugging strange Git behavior when the easy explanations stop working. Section 0 · Introduction Introduction Section I · Foundations Why Git gets slow, what Git stores, and how refs and the index steer through it. Why Git Performance Matters Git's Core Data Model Refs, HEAD, Reflogs, Index Section II · History and Rewrite How Git walks history and how rewrite commands reshape it without mutating commits. Revisions and History Traversal Merge, Rebase, Cherry-Pick, Rewrite Section III · Storage and Local Scale Object storage, index cost, maintenance, and the techniques that shrink local state. Loose Objects, Packfiles, Delta Compression The Index as a Performance Structure Commit-Graph, Bloom Filters, MIDX, Bitmaps Git GC and Maintenance Sparse-Checkout and Sparse-Index Section IV · Large-Repo Operations, Transport, and Scale Clone shape, transfer policy, parallel work with worktrees, repository size, and ref scale. Partial Clone and Promisor Remotes Scalar, Prefetch, Large Repositories Worktrees Clone, Fetch, Push, Protocol v2 Bundles and Bundle URIs Reducing Repository Size Large Ref Sets: Files, Packed-Refs, Reftable, and git refs Section V · Diagnosis and Recovery How to instrument Git, find the slow layer, apply high-leverage settings, and recover when the repository is actually wrong. Instrumenting Git Finding and Fixing Slow Git Configuration Playbook Recovery and Repair Back Matter Epilogue: Git in the Agent Loop Appendix: Compatibility Guidance Appendix: Approaches to Virtualized Working Trees Glossary of Git Terms Download the full book as a PDF

评论（默认中文）

hmpc 2026/04/28 17:03
同样，如果不注重性能，我可以全心全意地推荐 Building Git[0]，它将引导您使用 Ruby 构建自己的 git 克隆（尽管该语言并不重要）。

[0]: https://shop.jcoglan.com/building-git/

Similarly, if not performance-focused, I can wholeheartedly recommend Building Git[0], which walks you through building your own git clone in Ruby (although the language is immaterial).

[0]: https://shop.jcoglan.com/building-git/

在 HN 中查看
alxgsv 2026/04/28 16:40
我在使用代码时从未遇到过 git 性能问题。猜猜我的仓库没有被咬。但是当我尝试在我的宠物项目中使用 git 作为更改的版本化数据库时，我学到了很多关于索引、压缩等的知识。文章涵盖了很多内容并且非常有帮助！
I never faced git performance issues when working with code. Guess my repos weren't bit. But when I tried to use git as a versioned database of changes in my pet project, I learned a lot about indexes, compacting, etc. Article covers a lot and is very helpful!
在 HN 中查看
ergl 2026/04/28 17:09
惊讶，惊讶，又一篇法学硕士生成的废话出现在 HN 的首页上。

从第 1 章开始：

> 当 Git 变慢时，工程师会以不好的方式适应。他们不再提出历史可以回答的问题。他们批量工作以避免同步成本。他们让杂乱的分支存活更长时间，推迟清理，并将存储库视为稍微危险的东西。

从 https://gitperf.com/epilogue.html

> 一旦机器开始以机器节奏生成代码，本书中的模型就不会被破坏。变化的是节奏：更多的分支、更多的提交、更多的自动化以及更多的周围元数据。流量变得越来越大，使 Git 在压力下保持清晰的功能从“最好有”变成了“必需”。

> 这些看起来不再像是侧面优化。它们使机器规模的 Git 流量保持可用。

Surprise, surprise, another piece of LLM-generated slop on the front page of HN.

From chapter 1:

> When Git slows down, engineers adapt in bad ways. They stop asking questions the history could answer. They batch work to avoid sync cost. They keep messy branches alive longer, postpone cleanup, and treat the repository like something slightly dangerous.

From https://gitperf.com/epilogue.html

> Once machines start producing code at machine cadence, the model from this book does not break. What changes is the pace: more branches, more commits, more automation, and more surrounding metadata. The traffic gets louder, and the features that keep Git legible under pressure move from "nice to have" to "essential."

> These stop looking like side optimizations. They are what keep machine-scale Git traffic usable.

在 HN 中查看
nananana9 2026/04/28 15:46
Git 是行业标准，因为它为您提供了一个非常强大且简单的程序 使用。我们都隐约意识到内部结构很复杂，但用户体验足够干净和可用，复杂性通常不会泄露出去。

但是当这种情况发生时，我必须处理布隆过滤器、packfiles、维护 git 垃圾收集器或 rerere 清理，就是我将代码库切换到集中式 VCS 的那一天。

这些东西很值得学习；但它与我在日常工作中想要考虑的任何事情相去甚远。

Git is industry standard, because for what it give you it's a remarkably robust and simple program to use. We're all vaguely aware that the internals are complex, but the UX is clean and usable enough that the complexity usually doesn't leak out.

But the day this breaks down and I have to deal with bloom filters, packfiles, maintaining the git garbage collector or rerere cleanup, is the day I switch our codebase to a centralized VCS.

This stuff is cool to learn about; but it's 5 layers removed from anything I want to be thinking about in my day to day work.

在 HN 中查看
codesnik 2026/04/28 17:17
我认为恰恰相反。 Git 的内部非常简单，它的 ui 只是旋钮和杠杆来触及简单可靠的内部结构。这就是为什么对于某些人来说，这看起来很混乱 - 他们想要按钮“做我想做的事”（所有人和他们的需求都是不同的），而对于其他人来说，它是干净的 - 打开油门，发动机就会转速。
i think it is the other way around. Git is pretty simple internally, and its ui is just knobs and levers to reach into that simple reliable internal structure. This is why for some people it seems like a mess - they want button "do what I want" (and all people and their needs are different), and for other people it's clean - open the throttle, engine will rev.
在 HN 中查看
thfuran 2026/04/28 16:17
我非常确定 git 几乎完全是行业标准，因为 GitHub 的存在。我非常不同意用户体验是干净的。 cli 有点混乱。
I'm pretty sure git is industry standard almost entirely entirely because GitHub exists. I very much disagree that the UX is clean. The cli is more than a bit of a mess.
在 HN 中查看
stingraycharles 2026/04/28 16:24
> 我非常确定 git 几乎完全是行业标准，因为 GitHub 的存在。

不，我清楚地记得那一次，Github 在它已经占据领先地位大约一两年后才变得流行起来。

GitHub 之所以成为 GitHub，是因为 git 是胜利者。有支持 bazaar 和 Mercurial 之类的替代集线器，但 git 赢了，因为对于大多数人来说，Linus 和它背后的内核团队足以有理由信任它。

（我这么说是因为我是一个更喜欢 hg 而不是 git 的人）

> I'm pretty sure git is industry standard almost entirely entirely because GitHub exists.

Nah, I remember that time vividly, Github became a thing about a year or two after it was already very much taking the lead.

GitHub became GitHub because git was the winner. There were alternative hubs that supported bazaar and mercurial and whatnot, but git won because for most people, Linus and the kernel team being behind it was reason enough to trust it.

(and I say this as someone who liked hg more than git)

在 HN 中查看
BerislavLopac 2026/04/28 16:21
任何曾经使用过 Mercurial 的人都非常清楚一个好的版本控制工具 UX 是什么样的......
Anyone who has ever used Mercurial knows very well what a good versioning tool UX looks like...
在 HN 中查看
miroljub 2026/04/28 16:28
> 任何曾经使用过 Mercurial 的人都非常清楚一个好的版本控制工具 UX 是什么样的......

如此真实。我以前使用过 Mercurial，之前也使用过 Darcs，它帮助我意识到现有的最好的版本控制工具 UX 仍然是 Git 提供的工具。

PS：专业的还有CVS、SVN、Perforce、Clear Case，并尝试了Fossil。它们在可用性方面都无法接近 Git。

> Anyone who has ever used Mercurial knows very well what a good versioning tool UX looks like...

So true. I used Mercurial back in the day and also used Darcs before it, and it helped me realize that the best versioning tool UX that exists is still the one Git provides.

PS: Also CVS, SVN, Perforce, and Clear Case professionally, and gave a try to Fossil. None of them even close to Git usability-wise.

在 HN 中查看
anitil 2026/04/28 13:54
我只读到第二章，它已经解释了一些我多年来一直错过的管道细节。这太棒了
I'm only on to chapter two and already it's explained some plumbing details that I somehow have missed all these years. This is great
在 HN 中查看
normie3000 2026/04/28 12:11
> LFS 增加了自己的操作开销。

似乎每个远程触摸命令都需要几秒钟，即使是在一个非常小的存储库上。

> LFS adds its own operational overhead.

Seemingly seconds on every remote-touching command, even on a very small repo.

在 HN 中查看
Hendrikto 2026/04/28 15:42
更糟糕的是，在大约半年左右的时间里，我现在在使用 LFS 时必须使用 Yubikey 验证我的 ed25519-sk 密钥三次（！）。每一次推动。
What is worse is that for about half a year or so, I now have to authenticate my ed25519-sk key with my Yubikey thrice (!) when using LFS. On every push.
在 HN 中查看
fragmede 2026/04/28 17:06
他们没有使用 gitannex 是 NIH 犯下的一个错误。
That they didn't go with git annex was such a fit of NIH of a mistake.
在 HN 中查看
aa-jv 2026/04/28 16:04
我一直想看到一本为普通人描述 git 的书，并为他们提供了大量示例，告诉他们如何使用它来做富有成效的事情。

即使对于小型办公室，git 也非常有用。 整个生产线工作流程都可以用 git 来实现……只要人们学会有效地使用它即可。

它不仅仅是为了发展。 作家可以有效地使用它。 会计师也是。

让我感到有点恼火的是，Git 并没有被任何操作系统供应商合并到操作系统前端 UI 中。如果为普通人提供一种简单的方法来使用 git 管理计算机使用的时间线/历史记录，那将是革命性的。

I've always wanted to see a book that describes git for the common man and gives them tons of examples for how to use it to do productive things.

Even for a small office, git can be immensely useful. Entire production line workflows can be implemented with git .. if only folks would learn to use it productively.

Its not just for development. Writers can use it productively. Accountants too.

It always kind of irks me that Git hasn't just been folded into the OS front-end UI by any of the OS vendors .. it'd be so revolutionary to give common folks an easy way to manage the timeline/history of their computer use using git.

在 HN 中查看
awesan 2026/04/28 16:56
明显的原因是，作家、会计师等使用的大多数文件格式都是二进制文件，它们并不能从 git 中受益匪浅。
The obvious reason is that most file formats used by writers, accountants, etc. are binary files which do not very much benefit from git.
在 HN 中查看
fragmede 2026/04/28 17:08
如今，Microsoft Office 文件都是压缩的 XML，有标准和一切。
Microsoft Office files are zipped XML these days, there's a standard and everything.
在 HN 中查看
snthpy 2026/04/28 10:38
我一直想问这个：

为什么不是

    git clone --depth 1 ...
默认值？

我猜想，对于我克隆的至少 90% 的存储库，我只是想安装一些东西。即使对于其余的，我也可能会修改代码，但很少查看历史。如果我这样做，那么我可以在此时执行 `git fetch` 并在其余时间节省带宽和磁盘空间。

I've been wanting to ask this:

Why isn't

    git clone --depth 1 ...
the default?

I would guess that for at least 90% of the repos I clone, I just want to install something. Even for the rest, I might hack on the code but seldom look into the history. If I do then I could do a `git fetch` at that point and save the bandwidth and disk space the rest of the time.

在 HN 中查看
dwattttt 2026/04/28 10:56
一个问题：为什么 git 会参与其中？您不需要存储库。
A question: why is git involved at all in this? You don't want a repository.
在 HN 中查看
skydhash 2026/04/28 11:13
这！默认情况下有一个链接来下载源代码的 tarball。如果用户想要贡献（或检查开发版本），您可以添加一个到 vcs 的链接。
This! The default was to have a link to download a tarball of the source. And if the user wanted to contribute (or check the devel version), you would add a link to the vcs.
在 HN 中查看
kingstnap 2026/04/28 12:07
获取 git 存储库而不仅仅是 tarball 很有用。

A）您可以更新它们，因为您可以 git pull 来获取更改。

B) 如果你想在上面应用补丁，最好有版本控制，这样你就可以跟踪你所做的更改，如果你想重新建立基础，这尤其有用。

Grabbing git repos instead of just tarballs is useful.

A) You can update them, because you can git pull to fetch changes.

B) If you want to apply patches on top, its better to have version control so you can keep track of what you changed, especially useful if you want to rebase.

在 HN 中查看
eddythompson80 2026/04/28 12:01
我认为 gitignore 解决了传统 tarball 方法难以解决的问题。

下载 tarball 并运行 ./configure 或 make，在此处或那里编辑配置文件等，然后运行 ​​`make install` 是最常见的流程。现在，我发现自己经常编辑 Dockerfile 以使其符合我的喜好。使用 git 存储库，存储库的所有者已排除所有本地文件、构建缓存等，您可以继续拉取更新以存储并重新应用本地更改。对于 tarball，您必须重新弄清楚。丢失您的构建缓存（可能取决于语言），丢失您在这里或那里所做的更改等。

I think gitignore solves a problem that is hard to solve with the traditional tarball approach.

Downloading a tarball and running ./configure or make, editing a config file here or there, etc then running `make install` is the most common flow. Now days I find myself frequently editing the Dockerfile to make it to my liking. With a git repo, the owners of the repo have excluded all the local files, build caches, etc and you can keep pulling to get updates stashing and reapplying your local changes. With tarballs, you have to figure it out all over again. Lose your build cache (language dependent maybe), lose a change you made here or there, etc.

在 HN 中查看
joshka 2026/04/28 10:59
尝试使用 `git clone --filter=blob:none` 代替

https://github.blog/open-source/git/get-up-to-speed-with-par...

https://gitperf.com/chapter-11.html

try `git clone --filter=blob:none` instead

https://github.blog/open-source/git/get-up-to-speed-with-par...

https://gitperf.com/chapter-11.html

在 HN 中查看
snthpy 2026/04/28 16:39
谢谢。那太棒了！我特别喜欢它然后在需要时延迟加载 blob。

我想问是否有办法将其设置为默认值，但我想我只会设置一个别名，就像我每天使用的大多数子命令一样。

Thanks. That's great! I especially like that it then lazy loads the blobs as you need them.

I was going to ask if there's a way to set that as the default but I guess I'll just set up an alias like I have for most of the subcommands I use daily.

在 HN 中查看
jurakovic 2026/04/28 12:24
如果只有你一个人怎么办？ Git 不仅仅适合那些“只想安装一些东西”的人
What if that's only you? Git isn't made only for those who "just want to install something"
在 HN 中查看
[deleted] 2026/04/28 11:20 deleted

[已删除]

[deleted]

在 HN 中查看
aa-jv 2026/04/28 16:07
它不是默认设置，因为这对于使用更大存储库的 git 的开发人员来说会适得其反，这就是 git 最初的开始方式 - 例如，如果它是默认设置，那么您的克隆深度对于 Linux 内核开发人员来说将完全无用。
Its not the default because that'd be counter-productive to developers who use git with larger repositories, which is how git started life in the first place - your clone depth would be entirely useless for Linux kernel developers, for example, if it were default ..

which makes more sense i guess
